MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-17-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to Defendant's Counsel for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is ordered off calendar/fee paid. Proceedings reported by: Giselle Casey, CSR#8098. (212)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Compel Defendants To Provide Further Answers To From Interrogatories Without Objection; Order For Monetary Santions;
Off Calendar Jan-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Julie A. Esposito, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & moving party: A & J Top International
No other appearance.

Ruling:
Tentative Ruling adopted: Plaintiff A & J Top One International, Ltd.'s Motion To Compel Defendants To Provide Further Answers To Form Interrogatories Without Objection is moot as further responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. The court finds that the late responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Defendants in the amount of $1,140, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Compel Defendants Further Answers And Documents In Response To Plaintiff'S Demand For Production Of Documents; Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Julie Esposito, Esq. appeared for plaintiff & moving party: A & J Top One International
No other appearance.

Ruling:
Tentative Ruling adopted: Plaintiff A & J Top One International, Ltd.'s Motion To Compel Defendants To Provide Further Answers To Plaintiff's Demand For Production Of Documents Without Objection is granted. Defendants are ordered to respond further and to produce documents within 15 days of notice of this order. The incomplete responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Defendant the amount of $727.50, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice And Motion For Order To Compel Defts To Anwer Form Interrogatories, And Demand For Production Of Documents Without Objections; And Order That Requests For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted; And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-02-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Julie A. Esposito, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & moving party: A&J Top One International
No other appearance.

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff A. J. Top One International Ltd.'s Motion To Compel Defendants To Answer Form Interrogatories, Demand For Production Of Documents Without Objections, And Order That Requests For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted is denied as responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. The court finds that the late responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Defendant Delta King Enterprises, Inc. in the amount of $727.50, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
